title: Create and Run a Test Service on iSeries
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on iSeries
Deploying Server Pages and Web Services on iSeries
Create and Run a Test Service on iSeries
text: Create and Run a Test Service on iSeries For testing purposes, create a test service that can be used as a web service on iSeries, and a form that can call the service. If you want to compile the service directly to the iSeries, edit the IDE assignment file and redirect $RESOURCES_OUTPUT to the Uniface Router running on your iSeries, into the correct UAR. For example: [SETTINGS] ;resources_output my_uar.uar ; compile to UAR locally $resources_output $TCP:my_uar.uar ; compile to UAR on remote iSeries [PATHS] $TCP TCP:myAS400|user|password Copy Note: This setting should not be used when compiling the form. Alternatively, create a UAR file containing the service and its signature and transfer it to the iSeries after compilation. Create and Deploy the Service On Windows, start the Uniface IDE and create a modeled entity, some fields, and a key for the entity. It does not matter what the names are or how many fields you use. Create a service named WEBSRV with one operation named callin that contains the following simple ProcScript: operation callin params string parmin : in numeric parmout: out endparams parmout = $length(parmin) putmess "WEBSRV: %%parmin%%%" return (parmout) end; callin Copy Compile the service. The location of the compiled service depends on your configuration. If you have redirected $resources_output to the Uniface Router on the iSeries, the UAR containing the service will be created on your iSeries (file UAR, member WEBSRV, if you have not specified another name in wasv.asn ). If you have not redirected compilation to the Uniface Router on the iSeries, transfer the it binary to your iSeries machine using FTP or drag-and-drop onto an IFS share. The use the Uniface Resource Manager ( urm ) on the iSeries to copy it from the IFS into the physical file member; this ensures that the end-of-file marker is placed in the last record (which FTP and other copy tools do not do). Create the Form and Test the Components If you directed the $RESOURCES_OUTPUT setting to the Uniface Router on iSeries when compiling the service, reverse this before compiling the form. For a front-end to call the service, create a form with three edit boxes named TF1, TF2 and D1, and a command button. In the detail trigger of the command button, add the following ProcScript: activate "WEBSRV".callin(TF1, TF2) D1=$status Copy Compile and test this form, which activates the service on the iSeries because you have redirected the activate of the WEBSRV service to your iSeries. In the TF1 field, enter an arbitrary string and click the command button. Using WRKACTJOB, press F5 repeatedly and notice that a USERVER instance is started on your iSeries. After a few seconds, TF2 and D1 should indicate the length of the string you entered. Note: After (re)compiling the service or its signature, or after transferring a new version of the UAR to the iSeries, restart the Uniface Router so that Uniface Servers that are using the older version go away, and new Uniface Servers only uses the new version. If this all works, then you have proven that Uniface services will correctly execute with the connection string you entered in web.xml . If not, find out what the problem is and fix it, because if it does not work this way, it will also not work for a web services client application. If you find that you need to change the $WASV path in the IDE assignment file to get it to work, apply the same change in web.xml as well. Note: If you make changes to web.xml , the wrd.war file be must recreated and re-installed into your web application server. For more information, see Configure the WRD and Create a wrd.war File on iSeries and Install the WRD in the Application Server . Related Links $RESOURCES_OUTPUT Specifies the location to which Uniface compiles objects, and from which objects are read by the Uniface test environment. Uniface Resource Manager (URM) The Uniface Resource Manager (URM) is a command-line utility for manipulating Uniface application resources. It features zip file manipulation functions and support for the standardized archive directory structure and file redirection mechanisms. urm Executable for the Uniface Resource Manager, a command line utility for creating and manipulating Uniface archive files and other resources, files and DBMS tables.
